name: Stripe
problem: Online payment infrastructure too complex
solution: Developer-first payment APIs
traction: $2M ARR, 100+ customers at Series A
founder_signal: Collison brothers, prev YC founders, technical depth
sequoia_thesis: API-first infrastructure play, high technical moat

name: Airbnb
problem: Hotels are expensive and lack local authenticity
solution: Marketplace for short-term home rentals
traction: 80,000 attendees targeted for DNC launch; early growth hack on Craigslist
founder_signal: Brian Chesky & Joe Gebbia, design-centric, high-grit (sold cereal to fund)
sequoia_thesis: Community-driven marketplace with global network effects

name: DoorDash
problem: Local merchants lack delivery infrastructure
solution: On-demand logistics platform for restaurants
traction: Started with Palo Alto focus; rapid suburban density win
founder_signal: Tony Xu, operational obsession, customer-first culture
sequoia_thesis: Efficiency-led logistics play focused on underserved suburban markets

name: Snowflake
problem: Legacy data warehouses weren't built for the cloud
solution: Cloud-native data platform with decoupled storage and compute
traction: High product-market fit among enterprise data teams
founder_signal: Former Oracle experts; later led by Frank Slootman
sequoia_thesis: Architecture-first advantage in the migration to the cloud

name: WhatsApp
problem: SMS is expensive and restricted to carriers
solution: Cross-platform mobile messaging via data/internet
traction: Millions of daily active users with zero marketing spend
founder_signal: Jan Koum & Brian Acton, focus on simplicity and privacy
sequoia_thesis: Viral utility platform with unprecedented user engagement

name: Instacart
problem: Grocery shopping is time-consuming and manual
solution: On-demand grocery delivery marketplace
traction: Strong early adoption in high-density urban markets
founder_signal: Apoorva Mehta, logistical expertise, prev Amazon engineer
sequoia_thesis: Asset-light logistics network for essential goods

name: Vanta
problem: Security compliance (SOC 2) is manual and slow
solution: Automated security monitoring and compliance platform
traction: High demand from SaaS companies needing to sell to enterprise
founder_signal: Christina Cacioppo, deep understanding of SaaS pain points
sequoia_thesis: Category creator in automated trust and security infrastructure

name: Nubank
problem: Banking in Brazil is bureaucratic and high-fee
solution: Digital-first mobile banking without fees
traction: Millions of waitlisted users; explosive growth in LATAM
founder_signal: David Vélez, local market expertise and strategic vision
sequoia_thesis: Disruptive digital platform for unbanked and overcharged populations

name: Anthropic
problem: AI models are becoming powerful but unreliable/unsafe
solution: AI safety and research company building steerable models
traction: Launch of Claude models; massive enterprise partnerships
founder_signal: Dario Amodei, technical leadership from OpenAI roots
sequoia_thesis: Safety-first approach is the prerequisite for enterprise AI adoption

name: Linear
problem: Existing issue trackers (Jira) are slow and bloated
solution: High-performance, keyboard-centric developer tools
traction: Cult-like following among top engineering teams
founder_signal: Karri Saarinen, former Airbnb/Coinbase design lead
sequoia_thesis: Product-led growth driven by superior UX/DX

name: Retool
problem: Building internal tools from scratch is repetitive and costly
solution: Low-code platform for building internal applications
traction: Broad adoption across engineering and ops teams
founder_signal: David Hsu, highly technical, focused on developer productivity
sequoia_thesis: The standard GUI for database and API interaction

name: Klarna
problem: Online checkout friction and lack of consumer credit
solution: Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) and simplified payments
traction: Dominance in European e-commerce markets
founder_signal: Sebastian Siemiatkowski, vision for global retail banking
sequoia_thesis: Removing transaction friction increases merchant conversion

name: SpaceX
problem: Access to space is prohibitively expensive
solution: Reusable rockets and vertically integrated aerospace
traction: First private company to reach orbit; NASA contracts
market: Aerospace and Satellite Internet, $1T+ long-term TAM
founder_signal: Elon Musk, extreme ambition and engineering rigor
sequoia_thesis: Fundamental cost reduction in launch enables new orbital economies

name: Figma
problem: Design tools were offline, siloed, and non-collaborative
solution: Browser-based, multiplayer design and prototyping
traction: Rapid takeover of design teams from Adobe/Sketch
founder_signal: Dylan Field, visionary on web-based creative software
sequoia_thesis: Multiplayer-first workflows are the future of all creative work

name: Unity
problem: Game development is difficult and platform-specific
solution: Cross-platform game engine and development tools
traction: Became the engine for the majority of mobile games
founder_signal: David Helgason, focused on democratizing game dev
sequoia_thesis: Horizontal platform play for the 3D content explosion

name: Attentive
problem: Email marketing is saturated and has low engagement
solution: Personalized SMS marketing platform for brands
traction: Explosive revenue growth among e-commerce retailers
founder_signal: Former TapCommerce founders, serial exit success
sequoia_thesis: SMS is the next high-intent channel for consumer brands

name: Blockaid
problem: Web3 is rife with fraud, phishing, and hacks
solution: Security layer that validates transactions before execution
traction: Integrated into major wallets (MetaMask) at launch
founder_signal: Israeli intelligence backgrounds (Unit 8200)
sequoia_thesis: Infrastructure-level security is required for mass Web3 adoption

name: Harvey
problem: Legal research and drafting are manual and expensive
solution: AI-powered platform built for professional legal services
traction: Partnerships with elite global law firms (PwC, A&O)
founder_signal: Winston & Gabriel, blend of legal and AI expertise
sequoia_thesis: Generative AI applied to high-value knowledge work

name: HubSpot
problem: Small businesses lack integrated marketing tools
solution: Inbound marketing and CRM platform
traction: High adoption in the mid-market SME segment
founder_signal: Dharmesh Shah & Brian Halligan, category creators
sequoia_thesis: SMBs need a unified 'front-office' operating system

name: Tessian
problem: Human error (misdirected emails) is a top security risk
solution: Machine learning to prevent data breaches via email
traction: Strong uptake in highly regulated financial/legal sectors
founder_signal: Imperial College engineers, focus on 'human layer' security
sequoia_thesis: Modern security must focus on human behavior, not just code

name: Weights & Biases
problem: ML experiments are hard to track, reproduce, and manage
solution: Developer-first MLOps platform for tracking experiments
traction: Used by top AI labs and enterprise ML teams
founder_signal: Lukas Biewald, founder of CrowdFlower, deep AI roots
sequoia_thesis: The 'GitHub for Machine Learning'

name: Nvidia
problem: PC graphics were slow and lacked 3D capability
solution: The Graphics Processing Unit (GPU)
traction: Initial wins in the gaming market; later AI dominance
founder_signal: Jensen Huang, visionary engineering leader
sequoia_thesis: Accelerated computing will redefine all visual and data processing

name: Palo Alto Networks
problem: Legacy firewalls were port-based, not app-aware
solution: Next-generation firewalls that understand applications
traction: Rapid replacement of legacy Cisco/Check Point hardware
founder_signal: Nir Zuk, world-class security architect
sequoia_thesis: Security needs to evolve to the application layer

name: ServiceNow
problem: IT service management was stuck in clunky legacy software
solution: Cloud-based IT workflow and service automation
traction: Rapid displacement of BMC and HP in the enterprise
founder_signal: Fred Luddy, deep domain expertise and product focus
sequoia_thesis: The 'Operating System' for the modern enterprise IT department

name: Block (Square)
problem: Small merchants can't accept credit card payments easily
solution: Mobile card reader and integrated POS software
traction: Viral adoption among local cafes and small shops
founder_signal: Jack Dorsey, product-led visionary, prev Twitter
sequoia_thesis: Democratizing financial services for the 'underserved' merchant
